note_data.title: Urbanisation
keyPoints: **Urbanisation** is the increase in the proportion of people living in urban areas compared to rural areas
**Push factors** drive people from rural areas including lack of employment, poor healthcare, natural disasters, and agricultural mechanisation
**Pull factors** attract people to urban areas including better job opportunities, improved services, education, and entertainment facilities
**Megacities** are urban areas with populations over 10 million people, such as Karachi which has over 16 million inhabitants
**Squatter settlements** are illegal housing areas built on land not owned by the inhabitants, often using temporary materials
**Shanty towns** develop due to rapid rural-urban migration when formal housing cannot meet demand, lacking basic services like water and electricity
Urban growth creates **housing shortages** leading to overcrowding and development of informal settlements on marginal land
**Traffic congestion** increases in cities due to more vehicles, inadequate road infrastructure, and poor public transport systems
Urban areas face **pollution problems** including air pollution from vehicles and industries, and water pollution from inadequate sewage treatment

definitions.definition: The increase in the proportion of people living in urban areas compared to rural areas
An urban area with a population of over 10 million people
Illegal housing built on land not owned by the inhabitants, using temporary materials and lacking basic services
The movement of people from countryside areas to towns and cities
An area of poor quality housing, lacking in amenities such as water supply, sewerage and electricity

howToAnswer.instruction: Give one fact in one sentence. No explanation needed.
Give the point + reason using 'because' or 'this is because'
Give detailed characteristics or features - what you can see
Give a logical reason or solution based on the evidence provided

modelAnswer.question: Explain why people migrate from rural areas to cities in developing countries. [6]
modelAnswer.answer: People migrate due to push factors from rural areas. Agricultural mechanisation reduces the need for farm workers, so unemployment increases. Natural disasters such as floods destroy crops and homes, forcing people to move. Rural areas often lack healthcare facilities and schools, making life difficult. Pull factors attract people to cities. Cities offer more employment opportunities in factories and services. Better healthcare and education facilities are available in urban areas. Cities provide entertainment and modern lifestyle opportunities that rural areas cannot offer.
markBreakdown: Mark 2: Push factor - natural disasters/poor services

commonMistakes: Never say 'people want better lives' - always specify what attracts them (employment, services, education)
Students often forget to distinguish between push and pull factors - always identify which type
Don't just list problems of cities - explain why these problems occur due to rapid urbanisation
Never say 'squatters are lazy' - explain they have no choice due to housing shortages and poverty
Students confuse shanty towns with slums - shanty towns are temporary, slums can be permanent but poor quality
examinerTips: This topic appears almost every year in Paper 2 - often linked with population questions
Always use specific examples like Karachi, Lahore or Orangi Town for Pakistani context
Case study of a city in a developing country is frequently examined - learn Karachi well
Problems and solutions questions are common - learn both negative and positive impacts
note_data.keyDiagramDescription: Urban land use model showing CBD in center, surrounded by inner city (including squatter settlements), suburbs, and rural-urban fringe. Include transport routes and direction of urban growth.
